# Getting Started [Install kaskaid and render your first masonry grid]

## Overview

kaskaid is a masonry layout for React with built-in windowing (virtualization), powered by [`@tanstack/react-virtual`](https://tanstack.com/virtual). Only the items in and around the viewport are rendered, so grids with thousands of items stay fast.

It ships in two flavors:

- **`<Masonry />`** — a ready-made component that renders the masonry grid for you.
- **`useMasonry`** — a headless hook that returns prop bags you spread onto your own JSX.

## Installation

Install the package along with its peer dependency:

:::code-group

```bash [pnpm]
pnpm add kaskaid @tanstack/react-virtual
```

```bash [npm]
npm install kaskaid @tanstack/react-virtual
```

```bash [yarn]
yarn add kaskaid @tanstack/react-virtual
```

```bash [bun]
bun add kaskaid @tanstack/react-virtual
```

:::

## Quick Start

Pass your items as `data` and render each one with `renderItem`:

```tsx twoslash
// @twoslash-cache: {"v":1,"hash":"96188a375f9b5ea24219b3c98a9264d275a214c6d5e8ba0e1c933d2f9c4cd179","data":""}
import { Masonry } from 'kaskaid';

const DATA = [200, 600, 200, 400, 100, 600, 200, 400, 100, 600];

function App() {
  return (
    <Masonry
      data={DATA}
      estimateSize={() => 400}
      renderItem={({ item, index }) => <div style={{ height: item }}>{index}</div>}
    />
  );
}
```

`estimateSize` is optional in client-only mode — items are measured after mount. It is still recommended for large lists so off-screen items contribute to the total scroll size and lane balancing.

## Lane Count

The lane (column) count is driven entirely by CSS: the library reads a `--lanes` custom property from the grid root, which it tags with `data-kaskaid-grid`. Media queries, container queries, or any other CSS mechanism can change it — no resize listeners in your code. See [Styling & Responsive Lanes](/docs/styling).

## Next Steps

Each guide shows the same task twice: once with the `<Masonry />` component, once with the `useMasonry` hook.

- [Infinite Loading](/docs/infinite-loading) — fetch the next page as the grid scrolls.
- [Scrolling in a Container](/docs/scrolling-in-a-container) — virtualize inside a panel instead of the page.
- [Server-Side Rendering](/docs/ssr) — ship pre-positioned items in the server HTML.
- [Imperative Scrolling](/docs/imperative-scrolling) — jump to an item by index.
- [Styling & Responsive Lanes](/docs/styling) — selectors, lane count, container queries.

Or jump straight to the [`<Masonry />`](/docs/api/masonry) props, the [`useMasonry`](/docs/api/use-masonry) hook, or see it running on the [Anatomy](/anatomy) page.
